metadata.title:
BREWER AND PASTOR
TRAVEL TOGETHER
ON BOOZE INVES-


TIGATION.

pages.raw_text:
NORTH YAKIMA, Jan. 29-John
Schlotfeldt, the North Yakima
brewer, and Rev. Morton l.. Rose,
of the North Yakima Christian
church, who went to Pendleton to
ascertain from first hand investi-
cations the effect that prohibition
had on the business conditions Of
that city, have returned. Rev
Rose said that the pictures and
statements made by the vets"
had been grossly exaggerated; Mr..
Schlotfeldt says that not all the
truth was told.


He says that not only are there
as many vacant buildings as was
shown, but there has been a large
decrease all down the line in rents
and values. He says that he found
that number of people who had
worked the hardest for local op-
ton had left Pendleton as soon as
they found out how disastrous were
the effects of prohibition. He says
